subject: Sociology
title: What Is Culture?
description: A quick, practical guide to What Is Culture?.

hook.text: Fish don't notice water. Humans don't notice culture. It surrounds everything yet remains invisible.

buildup.text: Culture includes shared beliefs, values, norms, symbols, and practices that shape how a group lives.

discovery.text: Sociologists distinguish material culture — objects and technology — from nonmaterial culture — ideas and beliefs.

twist.text: Culture feels natural, but it's learned. Children raised in different cultures develop entirely different worldviews.

climax.text: Culture isn't decoration. It's the invisible operating system that programs how we think, feel, and act.

punchline.text: Culture is the water we swim in.
